raw.title: Masonry — Development & Lap Splice Length
raw.body_text: Project 💾 Save📂 Load 🖨 Summary📄 Full Calc⊞ Expand All⊝ Collapse Masonry — Development & Lap Splice Length 🖨 Print / PDF Masonry — Development & Lap Splice Length Deformed bar development length and Class A lap splice per TMS 402-22 Eq. 6-2 (MDG REK-10) TMS 402-22 ASD & Strength Design §6.1.6.3.1 Development Length §6.1.7.1.2 Lap Splice Eq. 6-2 MDG 2022 REK-10 K₁ = 3.500" t = 7.625" Open cell Grouted cell (reinf.) Face 1 Face 2 Horizontal Section — K Geometry K₃ = 9dᵇ = 9 × 0.625" = 5.625" ℓd = 19.50" t = 7.625" Foundation dowel Wall bar Grouted cell (reinf.) Foundation / Footing Elevation — Lap Splice Detail TMS 402-22 Eq. 6-2: ℓd = 0.13db² fyγ / (K√f’m) ≥ 12" 1 — Masonry Properties Masonry Compressive Strength, f'm (psi) Nominal Wall Thickness (in) 4 in. nominal (3.63 in. actual) 6 in. nominal (5.63 in. actual) 8 in. nominal (7.63 in. actual) 10 in. nominal (9.63 in. actual) 12 in. nominal (11.63 in. actual) 16 in. nominal (15.63 in. actual) Actual thickness: 7.63 in. 2 — Reinforcement Properties Bar Size #3 (db = 0.375 in.) #4 (db = 0.500 in.) #5 (db = 0.625 in.) #6 (db = 0.750 in.) #7 (db = 0.875 in.) #8 (db = 1.000 in.) #9 (db = 1.128 in.) #10 (db = 1.270 in.) #11 (db = 1.410 in.) Steel Yield Strength, fy (psi) Grade 60 — 60,000 psi Grade 75 — 75,000 psi Grade 40 — 40,000 psi fy Override (psi) Overrides dropdown if different Bar Size Factor, γ Auto: 1.0 for #3–#5 | 1.3 for #6–#7 | 1.5 for #8+ → γ = 1.0 for #3–#5 3 — K Factor Geometry K = min(Cover, Clear Spacing, 9db) Per TMS 402-22 §6.1.6.3.1, K is the least of: (1) masonry cover to bar surface, (2) clear spacing between adjacent bars, and (3) 9db. Set clear spacing to a large value (e.g. 999) if there is only one bar or bars are widely spaced. Bar Position Centered in wall (auto cover) Custom clear cover Masonry Clear Cover, K1 (in) — auto-calculated = (tactual − db) / 2 for centered bar Masonry Clear Cover, K1 (in) — enter manually Clear distance from bar surface to nearest masonry face Clear Spacing Between Bars, K2 (in) Enter 999 for single bar / widely spaced (not governing) ▶ Calculate Development & Splice Length ✓ #5 Bar — Required Development / Splice Length: 19.50 in. (19.47 in. raw, rounded up) K = 3.50 in. (K₁ — Masonry Clear Cover) | ℓd from Eq. 6-2 = 19.47 in. | Required = max(19.47, 12.0) = 19.47 in. K FACTOR — GOVERNING CANDIDATE K₁ — Masonry Clear Cover ▶ GOVERNS 3.50 in. (t−dᵇ)/2 = (7.63−0.63)/2 K₂ — Clear Bar Spacing 999.00 in. Single bar / not applicable K₃ — 9dᵇ 5.63 in. 9 × 0.63 in. 3.50 in. K (governing) 19.47 in. ℓd from Eq. 6-2 12.00 in. Minimum (§6.1.6.3.1) 19.50 in. Required ℓd = Splice Length (rounded up to ¼ in.) INPUT SUMMARY Property Value Bar size #5 (db = 0.63 in., Ab = 0.31 in.²) Steel yield strength, fy 60,000 psi Bar size factor, γ 1.0 (auto for #5) Masonry f'm 2,000 psi Wall actual thickness, t 7.63 in. (nominal 8 in.) Masonry clear cover, K1 3.50 in. (centered bar, auto) Clear bar spacing, K2 999 in. (single bar / N/A) 9db, K3 5.63 in. Governing K 3.50 in. (K₁ — Masonry Clear Cover) Check Reference Computed Minimum Status Detail Development Length ℓd K = 3.50 in. (K₁ — Masonry Clear Cover) TMS 402-22 §6.1.6.3.1 Eq. 6-2 19.47 in. 12.0 in. OK ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §6.1.6.3.1, Eq. 6-2 — Development Length of Deformed Bar in Tension Eq. 6-2: ℓ d = 0.13 db2 fy γ K √f'm = 0.13 × 0.632 × 60,000 × 1.0 3.50 × √2000 psi = 3046.9 3.50 × 44.72 = 19.47 in. K calculation: K 1 (cover) = 3.50 in. = (7.63−0.63)/2 K 2 (clear spacing) = ∞ (single bar) K 3 = 9d b = 9 × 0.63 = 5.63 in. K = min(3.50, ∞, 5.63) = 3.50 in. ℓ d,calc = 19.47 in. > 12 in. min ✓ ℓd = max(19.47, 12.0) = 19.47 in. Lap Splice Length lsplice Class A splice = ℓd; practical = 19.50 in. TMS 402-22 §6.1.7.1.2.1 19.47 in. 12.0 in. OK ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §6.1.7.1.2 — Class A Lap Splice in Tension Per TMS 402-22 §6.1.7.1.2, the lap splice length for a Class A splice equals the development length: l splice = ℓ d ≥ 12 in. (Eq. 6-2 governs) lsplice = 19.47 in. Practical (rounded up to ¼ in.): 19.50 in. = 19 in. + 2/4 in. Engineering Notes: Development length per TMS 402-22 Eq. 6-2 applies to deformed bars in tension. For compression development, see TMS 402-22 §6.1.6.4 (different formula). K is limited to a maximum of 9db regardless of actual cover or spacing. For bars #6 and larger (γ = 1.3 or 1.5), the longer splice lengths often require two or more CMU courses. Verify that field conditions accommodate the computed length. For foundation dowels, the splice begins at the top of the foundation or above the flashing/bond-break plane. Ensure the dowel is embedded into the foundation for the same computed lap length (or per separate footing anchorage check). Standard hooks may reduce the required development length per TMS 402-22 §6.1.6.3.2. Hooked bar development is not computed here. Minimum lap splice for TMS 402-22 §6.1.7.1.2.1: 12 in. or ℓd, whichever is greater. This calculator uses max(ℓd,Eq.6-2, 12 in.). ⇄ Results Hub ⇄ Results Hub×

audit.dimensions: PASS: t=7.625 in, K=5.625 in, ld=19.50 in match fixture/math
audit.note: post-fix re-judgment 2026-07-11 (Claude reviewer): all required elements present in rebuilt diagram; screenshots in tools/masonry-audit/diagram-review/. Pre-fix failure retained in git history (commit 9c3596f).
